summary: Recent updates to the Firefox browser's integration of **WebRTC**, the technology behind real-time video calls, have focused on enhancing memory management and efficiency. Key changes include replacing older memory allocation methods with more modern practices, ensuring that video encoder and decoder components return smart pointers. Specifically, the encoder and decoder for both standard media and GmpVideoCodec have been updated to utilize `std::unique_ptr`, which helps manage the lifecycle of memory automatically and reduce the likelihood of memory leaks. These modifications help improve performance and reliability in video communication, making online interactions smoother for users._No visible change_ is expected in the user experience immediately, but these underlying improvements pave the way for more robust features in the future.
